titleStmt.title: Letter from Charles P. O'Neill to Matthew Nathan, 30 Mar 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles P. O'Neill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Charles P. O’Neill, a solicitor based in Dublin to the under-secretary for Ireland, Sir Mathew Nathan (1862–1939). Mathew Nathan had become under-secretary in 1914 under Augustine Birrell (1850–1933). After the Easter Rising 1916, Nathan resigned due to his misjudgement and failure to stop the Rising. The letter is a request by Charles P. O’Neill to be considered for the vacant position of Chief Crown Solicitor, which had become available after the death of Malachy J. Kelly. In the letter he outlines his previous experience in various public positions and goes on to claim he has the support of the members of parliament for the city of Dublin.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Chambers, 36 Dawson Street, Dublin 30 March 1916 Sir I beg to offer myself as a Candidate
for the position of Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland. I was
admitted a solicitor in the Easter Term 1892 - just 24
years ago, and have continuously practised my
profession since that time. I am and have for many years been
acting for several Public Bodies including
The Blackrock Urban Council Co Dublin
The Rathdown Rural Council Co Wicklow
The grangeBoard , and also acted
as Law agent for the Kilmainham Urban
District Council before that District was taken
into the City. I have had experience of every branch of a Solicitor's Practice,
from the Kelly Sessions to the House of Lord's
and have also had during the past 17 years a
considerable amount of business before the
Committees of both Houses of Parliament I herewith enclose copy of letter from
my old friend Mr John Clancy K.C. MP for
North Dublin expressing his opinion as to my
professional standing. I have the honour of having the support
of all the members of Parliament for the City and
County of Dublin and also of Mr T P O' Connor MP.
&Joseph Devlin MP and other members of
Parliament. I propose to obtain and send
you letters from some of the judges of the
High Court and from leading members of the
Bar who know me. I have been Chairman of the Pembroke
Urban District for the past five years and
am acting as Local Controller of Recruiting
in that District. I am Sir
Your obedient servant Charles P O' Neill The Right Honourable
Sir Matthew Nathan
Dublin Castle
